title: Unable to run imported image rootless
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I've just saved an image that's working just fine to a tarball and then imported it and tried to run it but I keep getting errors.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman save archlinux -o archlinux.tar`

2. `podman import archlinux.tar archlinux2`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
[I] ➜ podman images
REPOSITORY                     TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED          SIZE
docker.io/library/archlinux2   latest   011937530344   15 minutes ago   10.8 GB
localhost/archlinux            latest   41e04dead9e9   7 hours ago      10.8 GB
localhost/archlinux/oblitum    latest   36d9c387c422   21 hours ago     9.4 GB
localhost/archlinux/base       latest   3ae3713c1bd0   23 hours ago     478 MB

[I] ➜ podman tag 011937530344 archlinux2

[I] ➜ podman rmi docker.io/library/archlinux2
Untagged: docker.io/library/archlinux2:latest

[I] ➜ podman images
REPOSITORY                    TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED          SIZE
localhost/archlinux2          latest   011937530344   17 minutes ago   10.8 GB
localhost/archlinux           latest   41e04dead9e9   7 hours ago      10.8 GB
localhost/archlinux/oblitum   latest   36d9c387c422   21 hours ago     9.4 GB
localhost/archlinux/base      latest   3ae3713c1bd0   23 hours ago     478 MB

[I] ➜ podman run --rm -it archlinux2
Error: No command specified on command line or as CMD or ENTRYPOINT in this image

[I] ➜ podman run --rm -it archlinux2 /bin/sh
Error: container_linux.go:345: starting container process caused "exec: \"/bin/sh\": stat /bin/sh: no such file or directory": OCI runtime error

[I] ➜ podman run --rm -it -u=francisco archlinux2 bash
Error: unable to find user francisco: no matching entries in passwd file
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

First, when importing while tagging it as archlinux2, I didn't expect the full tag to become `docker.io/library/archlinux2`, it doesn't make sense. Second, I ignore how to run the imported image as rootless.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[I] ➜ podman version
Version:            1.5.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.8
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
[I] ➜ podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.8
  podman version: 1.5.1
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.10.1
  Conmon:
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.0, commit: e217fdff82e0b1a6184a28c43043a4065083407f'
  Distribution:
    distribution: arch
    version: unknown
  MemFree: 13708578816
  MemTotal: 16747802624
  OCIRuntime:
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc8
      commit: 425e105d5a03fabd737a126ad93d62a9eeede87f
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: leibniz
  kernel: 5.2.9-arch1-1-ARCH
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 75h 12m 14.54s (Approximately 3.12 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/francisco/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /home/francisco/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 4
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
  VolumePath: /home/francisco/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[I] ➜ pacman -Qi podman
Name            : podman
Version         : 1.5.1-1
Description     : Tool and library for running OCI-based containers in pods
Architecture    : x86_64
URL             : https://github.com/containers/libpod
Licenses        : Apache
Groups          : None
Provides        : None
Depends On      : cni-plugins  conmon  device-mapper  iptables  libseccomp  ostree  runc  skopeo  btrfs-progs  slirp4netns  libsystemd
Optional Deps   : podman-docker: for Docker-compatible CLI
Required By     : None
Optional For    : None
Conflicts With  : None
Replaces        : None
Installed Size  : 102.71 MiB
Packager        : Bartłomiej Piotrowski <bpiotrowski@archlinux.org>
Build Date      : Sat 17 Aug 2019 05:16:49 PM -03
Install Date    : Sun 18 Aug 2019 04:17:14 AM -03
Install Reason  : Explicitly installed
Install Script  : No
Validated By    : Signature
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

physical

comments.body: My main interest in doing this is to carry the tarball to other machines, but the second is that I wish to delete the original image and leave the imported one because I want to check if by doing that I can erase the internal history and overlayfs diffs from filesystem and have the image squashed. I wish there was a squash command for doing this.
Oh sorry... it seems like I used the wrong command for images, it's `podman load`. And after loading, it just creates a new tag, so not useful for squashing.
@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat Can `buildah` easily squash? I think I recall that it can?
It might not be a bad idea to add a `buildah squash` command.  @nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat WDYT? I think there is a PR open on squashing a number of layers.

@rhatdan I think it's great having it, because diffs may include files that don't exist anymore in the result image, because they have been removed. A squash command would be nice to clear up any of such historical artifacts.
Could you open an issue with buildah to add support.

We've `--squash` already for `commit` and `bud`.  We also have a `--max-layers` PR in flight waiting for a review from @nalind (hint, hint) https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1790.  Just recovered my laptop after it crashed (yet again), I'll go play.
@rhatdan sure: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1810
@TomSweeneyRedHat I tried `--squash` when building initial image with `bud` but I've hit this issue, I couldn't use the flag at all: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1806.

Assuming `buildah commit --squash` is working, it's not solving this issue from what I can see quickly.
I was hoping the below would work.  @nalind any thoughts?
```
# podman pull alpine
# podman save alpine -o alpine.tar
# podman import alpine.tar alpine2

# podman images
REPOSITORY                  TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED          SIZE
docker.io/library/alpine2   latest   7e367ff8040b   52 seconds ago   5.85 MB
docker.io/library/alpine    latest   961769676411   25 hours ago     5.85 MB

# podman tag 7e367ff8040b alpine2

# podman rmi docker.io/library/alpine2
Untagged: docker.io/library/alpine2:latest

# podman images
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED              SIZE
localhost/alpine2          latest   7e367ff8040b   About a minute ago   5.85 MB
docker.io/library/alpine   latest   961769676411   25 hours ago         5.85 MB

# buildah from alpine2
alpine2-working-container

# buildah commit --squash alpine2-working-container alpine3

# podman images
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
localhost/alpine3          latest   b0e5f1aca6b0   5 seconds ago   5.85 MB
localhost/alpine2          latest   7e367ff8040b   6 minutes ago   5.85 MB
docker.io/library/alpine   latest   961769676411   25 hours ago    5.85 MB

# podman run --rm -it alpine3 /bin/sh
Error: time="2019-08-21T17:18:49-04:00" level=error msg="container_linux.go:346: starting container process caused \"exec: \\\"/bin/sh\\\": stat /bin/sh: no such file or directory\"\n"
container_linux.go:346: starting container process caused "exec: \"/bin/sh\": stat /bin/sh: no such file or directory"
: OCI runtime error
```





```

@TomSweeneyRedHat [as I commented before](https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3871#issuecomment-523597449), I get the same error when I use `import` from an image I've used `save`, the image works when I `load` the .tar, not `import` (like you did). If it's the same machine you used `save` and you didn't delete the original image, then it simply creates a new tag, otherwise it'll recreate the image.

I'm new to podman and buildah so I don't know exactly what you're attempting but I got the same error without buildah in the process.

@TomSweeneyRedHat I'm trying buildah like you did above (except for the `import` part), but currently I'm failing for completely different reasons, `buildah commit --squash` fails because there's no space left on disk, this seems to be happening because it's using `/tmp` as temporary storage, and my image is big. Any way for to change the temporary storage of the operation?

@rhatdan done https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1815.

`buildah info` shows `"RunRoot": "/run/user/1000"`, so I think it's not using it for temporary storage on this operation.

Folks, I've resized my `/tmp` to try this, gladly I had enough RAM for this attempt, and `buildah commit --squash` worked! After applying it and removing the original image, all overlayfs diff history was pruned from disk, and I earned a few megabytes in the process due to that.

So @TomSweeneyRedHat, as I [commented](https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3871#issuecomment-523952933), I guess the error message you got is indeed for using `import` instead of `load`.
@oblitum glad it worked out for you!  I'll poke further at that error message when I get a chance.

May I ask something here, thought I think it's off-topic but I ignore where to chat about podman. I've read that podman and docker containers/images are compatible, but I'm concerned whether an image created rootless will run fine in Docker for Windows, will it? My doubt comes due to the uid/gid mapping and what will happen on Windows.

@oblitum best asked on the Podman mailing list:  https://podman.io/mailinglist/

I've not tried what you're suggesting, but I think it would be a neat experiment, I'm going to guess you're spot on about the uid/gid mapping being "fun" in Windows, but it would be fun to try.  Others on the list might  have experience with that though.
@TomSweeneyRedHat OK, thanks.
OK, I've loaded the tarball on [docker for windows](https://github.com/docker/docker.github.io/issues/6910#issuecomment-403502065) and the image simply works :)
